message:
fix(hook): gate CARGO_TARGET_DIR sync on cargo kind and rewrite command for worker

Reworks the CARGO_TARGET_DIR forwarding so it's driven by the
compilation kind, not the user's env allowlist, and so the remote
worker always uses a clean `.rch-target` directory regardless of
what the local invocation set. Closes the bug where two cargo
projects with different CARGO_TARGET_DIR values fed to the same
worker would clobber each other's target directories.

Symptoms fixed:

- Local `cargo build` with `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/cargo-target`
  in the user's shell would forward that absolute path to the worker,
  which would then try to write into a path that doesn't exist on
  the worker's filesystem and fail late in the compile.
- Inline `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=foo cargo build` invocations would forward
  the variable both via env AND via the command string, producing
  duplicate / inconsistent directives at the worker.
- Users without `CARGO_TARGET_DIR` in their env_allowlist got NO
  worker-side `.rch-target` syncing at all, even for routine cargo
  builds that should always sync their target outputs back.

Changes:

- `resolve_forwarded_cargo_target_dir(_with_lookup)`:
  - Signature now takes `kind: Option<CompilationKind>` instead of
    `env_allowlist: &[String]`. Gate becomes: enabled iff the
    classification is one of the cargo kinds that actually emits a
    target/ directory (`CargoBuild`, `CargoCheck`, `CargoClippy`,
    `CargoDoc`, `CargoTest`, `CargoNextest`, `CargoBench`). The new
    `cargo_kind_uses_target_dir(kind)` helper centralises the list.
  - Source order flipped: command tokens are checked FIRST, env
    SECOND. Reasoning: an inline `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=foo cargo build`
    is the user's most-recently-stated intent, while a long-lived
    shell export is background context. The previous order made
    inline assignments invisible if env was set.
  - Empty / unset / absent now all collapse to "use the default
    `<invocation_cwd>/target`" instead of the previous "skip
    forwarding entirely". This guarantees a consistent target-dir
    sync for every cargo invocation.
  - Verbose log line rewritten to reflect the new flow:
    "Cargo target sync active; forcing worker
    CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.rch-target and syncing back to <local-path>".

- `rewrite_cargo_target_dir_command_for_remote(command, command_tokens,
  forwarded_cargo_target_dir, reporter)` (new):
  - Returns the command string to actually send to the worker. When
    target-dir forwarding is active, strips any inline
    `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=...` assignments from the command tokens
    (top-level, plus through `sudo`/`time`/`env --` wrapper
    prefixes via `strip_cargo_target_dir_assignments_from_command_tokens`).
    The worker then receives a clean command string and the
    `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=.rch-target` override comes purely from the
    pipeline's env_overrides, eliminating the duplicate-directive
    failure mode.
  - When target-dir forwarding is OFF, returns the command verbatim.

- `strip_cargo_target_dir_assignments_from_command_tokens(tokens)`
  (new): Walks past `sudo`, `time`, and `env [--]` wrapper prefixes,
  then removes every leading `KEY=VALUE` token whose KEY is
  `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`. Returns the stripped vector iff any removal
  happened. Mirrors the structure of the existing
  `extract_cargo_target_dir_from_command_tokens` for symmetry.

- `cargo_target_env_allowlist(env_allowlist, cargo_target_sync)`
  (new): Returns the user's env allowlist with `CARGO_TARGET_DIR`
  unconditionally appended whenever target-dir sync is active. The
  user no longer has to remember to add it manually for routine
  cargo builds — the hook handles it.

- `cargo_target_env_overrides(local_target_dir)` (new): Returns a
  `HashMap` containing `{ CARGO_TARGET_DIR: <local-path-string> }`
  ready to be applied via `TransferPipeline::with_env_overrides`.
  When target-dir sync is inactive, returns `None` and no override
  is applied.

- Two call sites in `run_exec` and `handle_selection_response`:
  - Switched to the new `kind`-based signature.
  - Build `remote_command` from `rewrite_cargo_target_dir_command_for_remote`
    and pass that (instead of the raw `command`) to
    `request_compilation_with_priority`, the retry block, and
    `execute_remote_compilation`.

- `execute_remote_compilation`:
  - Builds the effective allowlist via `cargo_target_env_allowlist`.
  - Builds env overrides via `cargo_target_env_overrides`.
  - Primary-pipeline construction now uses the effective allowlist
    AND, when overrides are present, threads them through
    `with_env_overrides(...)`.

- `rch/src/transfer.rs`:
  - Removes the `#[cfg(test)]` gate on `TransferPipeline::with_env_overrides`.
    The method was previously test-only because no production code
    called it; the new `execute_remote_compilation` path now does,
    so it must be available in non-test builds. The implementation
    is unchanged.
